scads 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    Quick Tutorial on scads

    Quick Tutorial on scads

    2024-10-31

    Introduction.Rmd

    Setup

    Confirm PolyFUN runs in your conda environment

    conda activate /dartfs/rc/lab/S/Szhao/liyang/conda/base_clone/

    Load R packages

    library(scads)
library(data.table)

    Load example data

    # Access the dataset
data("demo_count_matrix")

# Examine dataset
dim(demo_count_matrix)
#> [1]  100 1000
demo_count_matrix[1:5,1:5]
#>      chr5:130672416-130672916 chr4:8726737-8727237 chr15:76191731-76192231
#> [1,]                        7                    1                       7
#> [2,]                        3                    2                       1
#> [3,]                       27                    7                       3
#> [4,]                        1                    1                      11
#> [5,]                       13                   20                       4
#>      chr7:26118486-26118986 chr9:115662457-115662957
#> [1,]                      2                        5
#> [2,]                      1                        0
#> [3,]                      7                        8
#> [4,]                      3                        3
#> [5,]                     20                        4

    Prepare a GWAS summary statistics file

    data_file <- system.file("extdata", "SIM_sumstats_chr10.txt.gz", package = "scads")
sumstats <- fread(data_file, header = T)
#> Warning in fileAccess.default(pathT, mode = 2): file.access(..., mode=2) and
#> file(..., open="ab") gives different results (-1 != 0). Will use the file()
#> results: /tmp/RtmpIUODho/F
#> Warning in fileAccess.default(pathT, mode = 2): file.access(..., mode=2) and
#> file(..., open="ab") gives different results (-1 != 0). Will use the file()
#> results: /tmp/RtmpIUODho/Y
#> Warning in fileAccess.default(pathT, mode = 2): file.access(..., mode=2) and
#> file(..., open="ab") gives different results (-1 != 0). Will use the file()
#> results: /tmp/RtmpIUODho/T
head(sumstats)
#>      chr    pos        beta         se     a0     a1         snp       pval
#>    <int>  <int>       <num>      <num> <char> <char>      <char>      <num>
#> 1:    10 134706  0.05536500 0.05685897      C      T  rs55896525 0.33019827
#> 2:    10 136067 -0.02858211 0.04524603      C      T rs185642176 0.52758333
#> 3:    10 136104 -0.01931718 0.04723619      C      G rs141504207 0.68257926
#> 4:    10 140366 -0.04360189 0.02560643      C      T  rs10904045 0.08861778
#> 5:    10 141014 -0.03969239 0.02519304      G      A   rs6560828 0.11514118
#> 6:    10 141602  0.02325330 0.02690964      G      C  rs35849539 0.38752373
#>        zscore
#>         <num>
#> 1:  0.9737251
#> 2: -0.6317043
#> 3: -0.4089487
#> 4: -1.7027712
#> 5: -1.5755300
#> 6:  0.8641252

    Run scads

    1. Run fastTopics
    • fastTopics: fit model and obtain topics
    • fastTopics-DE: obtain peaks associated with topics
    • locfdr: adjust for multiple testing [issue with NA on some runs - TODO: FIX ISUE] (Current resolution: just run again)

    1. Create topic annotations for LDSC

    2. Run LDSC

    • Resolved issues - should be running though the enrichment results will not make sense
    1. Calculate Cell Score
    • Made up topic enrichment estimates (topic 2 is enriched)
    • Output list of 2
      1. out1 (fastTopics results)
      2. cs (cell scores)
    scads_res <- scads(demo_count_matrix, 
                     nTopics = 5, 
                     bed_dir = "/dartfs/rc/lab/S/Szhao/liyang/SCSviaTM/analysis/simu_20241028/bed_dir", 
                     sumstats_dir = "/dartfs/rc/lab/S/Szhao/liyang/SCSviaTM/analysis/simu_20241028", 
                     gwas_nsamps = 45000, 
                     gwas_trait = "SIM", 
                     outdir = "/dartfs/rc/lab/S/Szhao/liyang/SCSviaTM/analysis/simu_20241028/scads")

    On this page
